title: Quiz: Two-Qubit Gates
description: Test your understanding of two-qubit gates and entanglement

questions.question: What is CNOT|10⟩ (control on first qubit, target on second)?
questions.explanation: CNOT flips the target (second) qubit when the control (first) qubit is |1⟩. Since control is |1⟩, the target flips: |10⟩ → |11⟩.

questions.question: What is the dimension of a two-qubit unitary matrix?
questions.explanation: Two qubits span a 4-dimensional Hilbert space (|00⟩, |01⟩, |10⟩, |11⟩), so gates are 4×4 matrices.

questions.question: To apply Hadamard to the first qubit only in a two-qubit system, we use:
questions.explanation: H ⊗ I applies H to the first qubit and identity to the second. The tensor product ordering is (first qubit) ⊗ (second qubit).

questions.question: The Bell state |Φ+⟩ = (|00⟩ + |11⟩)/√2 can be written as a tensor product of two single-qubit states.
questions.explanation: False! |Φ+⟩ is entangled and cannot be written as |a⟩⊗|b⟩ for any single-qubit states |a⟩ and |b⟩.

questions.question: What is CZ|11⟩?
questions.explanation: The CZ gate applies a -1 phase only to |11⟩. CZ|11⟩ = -|11⟩.

questions.question: The SWAP gate transforms |01⟩ to:
questions.explanation: SWAP exchanges the two qubits: SWAP|01⟩ = |10⟩.

questions.question: To create the Bell state |Φ+⟩ from |00⟩, we apply:

options.text: H on first qubit, then CNOT
CNOT, then H on first qubit
